methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 26 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.0B - $15.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Waymo
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Pre-IPO), post-money $126B

data.narrative:
ElevenLabs is a pre-IPO AI & Software company currently valued at $3B+ following $500M in total capital raised. The valuation analysis relies on a peer-median approach across 26 same-sector companies at similar valuation tiers, supplemented by reference to a broader comp universe of 9 private AI/software businesses and 1 public comparable (Palantir).

The peer-median method yields a $4.0B implied valuation, derived from the median across the 26-company peer set with a stated range of $1.0B to $15.0B. Representative peers in this universe include Applied Intuition ($15.0B, Series F), Skild AI ($14.0B, Series C), Crusoe Energy ($10.0B, Series E), Neuralink ($9.7B private), and others positioned across seed through late-stage funding rounds. This method represents the only quantitatively calibrated triangulation applied to this target.

The single-method analysis supports a valuation range of $3.4B to $4.7B, with a median estimate of $4.0B. This implies modest upside from the current stated valuation, though the wide dispersion within the peer set ($1.0B–$15.0B) and reliance on a single methodology introduce material uncertainty. Stage-specific comparables and revenue or growth-rate multiples would strengthen conviction; their absence limits this analysis to peer-relative positioning only.
